title: lepidosperma fairallianum
intro: Lepidosperma fairallianum, also known as Fairall's spike rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Lepidosperma fairallianum is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into a tea or infusion, and it is also used in some commercial skincare products due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
